page_content:
NFSC386 Experiential Learning (3-6 Credits) Prerequisite: Permission of AGNR-Nutrition and Food Science department. Restriction: Junior standing or higher. Formerly: FDSC386 and NUTR386.

NFSC388 Honors Thesis Research (3-6 Credits) Undergraduate honors thesis research conducted under the direction of an AGNR faculty member in partial fulﬁllment of the requirements of the College of AGNR Honors Program. The thesis will be defended to a faculty committee. Restriction: Must be admitted to AGNR Honors Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

NFSC398 Seminar (1 Credit) Presentation and discussion of current literature and research in food science. Formerly: FDSC398.

NFSC421 Food Chemistry (3 Credits) Basic chemical and physical concepts are applied to the composition and properties of foods. Emphasis on the relationship of processing technology to the keeping quality, nutritional value, and acceptability of foods. Prerequisite: BCHM461.
